If a suggested fix looks wrong for my setup, say so instead of running it.\n\n- Settings (D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json › hooks.SessionStart.0.hooks.0.type): Invalid input\n- Settings (D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json › hooks.UserPromptSubmit.0.hooks.0.type): Invalid input\n- Settings (D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json › hooks.PreToolUse.0.hooks.0.type): Invalid input\n- Settings (D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json › hooks.PostToolUse.0.hooks.0.type): Invalid input\n- Settings (D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json › hooks.SubagentStop.0.hooks.0.type): Invalid input\n- Settings (D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json › hooks.Stop.0.hooks.0.type): Invalid input"},"uuid":"de798277-e016-4cd0-a71d-62eaf54a1237","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:04:58.321Z","permissionMode":"default","promptSource":"typed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"de798277-e016-4cd0-a71d-62eaf54a1237","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"71254b6e-9bf6-40ce-9915-4968d7b82706","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:04:58.320Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"69e944e1-c60c-4a8e-abc3-c609058ff1ac","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_1a4314e6af6b962897b0a318","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The `settings.json` file shows that the hook objects are missing the `type` field.\nThe `/doctor` output mentioned `Invalid input` for `hooks.SessionStart.0.hooks.0.type` and so on.\nIn Claude Code's `settings.json` schema for hooks, the `type` field is required to specify how the command should be executed (e.g., `shell`).\n\nLooking at the current entries:\n`{ \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true }`\n\nIt should likely be:\n`{ \"type\": \"shell\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true }`\n\nSince all these hooks are executing bash scripts, the `type` should be `\"shell\"`.\n\nI will propose adding `\"type\": \"shell\"` to each of these hook definitions.\n\nWait, I should verify the valid types for hooks. Typically they are `shell` or `mcp`. Since these are `bash` commands, `shell` is the correct one.\n\nThe fix will be to add `\"type\": \"shell\"` to every hook object in the `settings.json` file.\n\nI'll list the changes:\n- SessionStart: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- UserPromptSubmit: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- PreToolUse: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- PostToolUse: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- SubagentStop: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- Stop: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n\nI will now explain this to the user and ask for confirmation.","signature":""}],"usage":{"input_tokens":23033,"output_tokens":0}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"48796843-5398-40b7-b3f2-45761dab387f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:05:13.658Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"48796843-5398-40b7-b3f2-45761dab387f","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_1a4314e6af6b962897b0a318","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The issues reported by `/doctor` are caused by missing `type` fields in your `.claude/settings.json` hooks. Since all your hooks are running bash scripts, they should be set to `\"shell\"`.\n\n**Proposed Fix:**\nI will add `\"type\": \"shell\"` to each hook object in `D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json`.\n\n**Changes:**\n- `SessionStart`: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- `UserPromptSubmit`: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- `PreToolUse`: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- `PostToolUse`: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- `SubagentStop`: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n- `Stop`: add `\"type\": \"shell\"`\n\nShall I proceed with these changes?"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":21726,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":587,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"4dc1da7b-a654-4ef0-bf58-e7cf055f2773","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:05:15.681Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

Or perhaps the structure itself is nested too deeply.\n\nLet's check the current `settings.json` again.\nThe structure is:\n`\"SessionStart\": [ { \"hooks\": [ { ... } ] } ]`\n\nLooking at the error: `hooks.*.hooks.0.type`.\n`hooks.*` refers to the event (e.g., `SessionStart`).\n`.hooks.0` refers to the first element of the `hooks` array inside the object in that event's array.\n`.type` refers to the `type` property of that object.\n\nIf `\"shell\"` is invalid, I need to find what the valid types are.\n\nWait, if I look at the `update-config` skill description, it mentions \"hooks configured in settings.json\".\nI should probably check if there's any documentation or a way to determine the schema.\nSince I don't have a manual, I can try to guess or check common patterns.\nActually, often in these CLI tools, the type might be `\"bash\"` or `\"command\"` or it might be that the `type` field shouldn't be there if it's implicitly a shell command, but the error says `Invalid input` for the *value* of `type`.\n\nWait, let me look at the structure again.\n```json\n\"SessionStart\": [\n  { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"shell\", \"command\": \"...\", \"async\": true } ] }\n]\n```\nMaybe the `hooks` array is redundant?\nMaybe it should be:\n```json\n\"SessionStart\": [\n  { \"type\": \"shell\", \"command\": \"...\", \"async\": true }\n]\n```\nBut the error says `hooks.*.hooks.0.type`. This implies the harness *expects* an object with a `hooks` property, and the first element of *that* `hooks` array has an invalid `type`.\n\nWait, is it possible the `type` should be something else?\nCommon types: `shell`, `command`, `script`, `bash`.\nIf `shell` is invalid, let's try to see if I can find any clues.\n\nActually, let's try to use the `update-config` skill if it can help, or just try a common alternative.\nBut the user asked me to \"Determine the current hook schema expected by this version\".\n\nI don't have a schema file. I can try to search the codebase for any mentions of \"hooks\" or \"settings.json\" to see if there's any internal documentation or examples.\nBut this is a Claude Code CLI, the schema is likely internal to the CLI binary, not the project codebase.\n\nWait! I have the `update-config` skill. Maybe it knows the schema?\nThe `update-config` skill is for configuring the harness.\n\nLet's try a different approach. If `\"shell\"` is invalid, what else could it be?\nLooking at the structure again:\n`\"SessionStart\": [ { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"shell\", ... } ] } ]`\nThe error is `hooks.*.hooks.0.type: Invalid input`.\n\nMaybe the value should be `\"command\"`?\nOr maybe it should be `\"bash\"`?\n\nLet's try to use `Bash` to see if I can find any clues in the environment or if there's a help command for settings.\n`claude --help` or something? But I am *inside* Claude.\n\nActually, if I look at the `update-config` skill again, it says:\n\"Automated behaviors... require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these... Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json.\"\n\nI'll try to use the `update-config` skill to \"fix\" the hooks. If the skill implements the current schema, it should produce the correct format.\n\nWait, I can try to ask the `update-config` skill to add a simple hook and see what it writes to the file.\n\nHowever, the user's prompt is \"Determine the current hook schema expected by this version. Rewrite the file to the correct schema and explain each change before editing.\"\n\nIf I can't find a schema, I'll have to deduce it.\nLet's try to check if the `type` field is actually required and what the valid values are.\nActually, in many recent versions of this tool, the `type` might be omitted and it defaults to shell, or the structure changed.\nBut the error is specifically about the *value* of `type`.\n\nLet's try to use `Grep` to see if there are any other `.claude/settings.json` files in the environment or if there's any sample config.\nI'll also check `.claude/settings.local.json`.\n\nAnother possibility: the `type` field should be removed entirely if the command is just a string.\nBut the error is `Invalid input` for `type`. If it were missing and required, it would usually say `Missing required field`. `Invalid input` usually means the value provided doesn't match the allowed enum.\n\nWhat if the value is just `\"shell\"` but it should be `\"shell\"`? I already tried that.\nWait, did I? Yes, I wrote `\"type\": \"shell\"`.\n\nMaybe the value should be `\"command\"`?\n\nLet's try to find if there's any documentation in the project.\nThe project is `seo-command-center`.\n\nI'll start by searching for any strings like `\"shell\"` or `\"hooks\"` in the codebase to see if there are any examples of how they are used in other project settings.","signature":""}],"usage":{"input_tokens":22663,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":1522,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"91627f2a-0f57-4f41-910d-dc102b46a484","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:10:49.810Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
