HTTP + SSE on localhost:7700 -> the live cockpit that fills as issues are found.\n6\t\n7\tSTARTER: works end to end out of the box. Extend the detectors (seo/detector.py) and\n8\tthe fixes (the model-driven title rewriting / redirect map) during the Sprint.\n9\t\n10\tNeeds the MCP SDK to expose tools to Claude (`pip install mcp`); without it the dashboard\n11\tstill runs so you can use run.py. Standard library otherwise.\n12\t\"\"\"\n13\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n14\timport json, os, queue, threading, time\n15\tfrom http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer\n16\t\n17\tHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n18\tROOT = os.path.dirname(HERE)\n19\tDASH_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"dashboard\")\n20\tOUT_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"outputs\")\n21\tPORT = int(os.environ.get(\"SEO_PORT\", \"7700\"))\n22\tMODEL = os.environ.get(\"RADAR_MODEL\", \"qwen3.5:9b\")\n23\t\n24\timport sys\n25\tsys.path.insert(0, ROOT)\n26\tfrom seo import detector  # noqa: E402\n27\t\n28\tRUN = {\"site\": None, \"urls\": 0, \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None, \"status\": \"idle\"}\n29\t_subs: list[queue.Queue] = []\n30\t_lock = threading.Lock()\n31\t\n32\t\n33\tdef _emit(event, data):\n34\t    payload = json.dumps({\"event\": event, \"data\": data})\n35\t    with _lock:\n36\t        for q in list(_subs):\n37\t            try: q.put_nowait(payload)\n38\t            except Exception: pass\n39\t\n40\t\n41\t# ----- pipeline tools (importable by run.py without MCP) -----\n42\tdef seo_load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n43\t    rows = detector.load_rows(export_dir)\n44\t    RUN.update({\"rows\": rows, \"urls\": len(rows), \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None,\n45\t                \"site\": _guess_site(rows), \"status\": \"running\"})\n46\t    _emit(\"loaded\", {\"site\": RUN[\"site\"], \"urls\": len(rows)})\n47\t    return {\"urls\": len(rows), \"site\": RUN[\"site\"]}\n48\t\n49\t\n50\tdef _guess_site(rows):\n51\t    if not rows: return \"unknown\"\n52\t    addr = rows[0].get(\"Address\", \"\")\n53\t    try:\n54\t        from urllib.parse import urlparse\n55\t        return urlparse(addr).netloc or \"unknown\"\n56\t    except Exception:\n57\t        return \"unknown\"\n58\t\n59\t\n60\tdef seo_detect() -> dict:\n61\t    issues = detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n62\t    RUN[\"issues\"] = issues\n63\t    RUN[\"summary\"] = detector.summarize(issues)\n64\t    for i in issues:\n65\t        _emit(\"issue\", i)\n66\t    _emit(\"summary\", RUN[\"summary\"])\n67\t    return {\"detected\": len(issues), \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"]}\n68\t\n69\t\n70\tdef _report_obj() -> dict:\n71\t    return {\n72\t        \"site\": RUN[\"site\"],\n73\t        \"urls_crawled\": RUN[\"urls\"],\n74\t        \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"] or {\"total_issues\": 0, \"by_severity\": {}},\n75\t        \"issues\": RUN[\"issues\"],\n76\t        \"fixes\": RUN.get(\"fixes\", {\"titles\": [], \"redirect_map\": []}),\n77\t        \"recommendations\": RUN.get(\"recommendations\", []),\n78\t        \"run_meta\": {\"model\": MODEL, \"model_calls\": RUN.get(\"model_calls\", 0),\n79\t                     \"duration_sec\": RUN.get(\"duration_sec\", 0)},\n80\t    }\n81\t\n82\t\n83\tdef seo_set_fixes(titles=None, redirect_map=None) -> dict:\n84\t    RUN[\"fixes\"] = {\"titles\": titles or [], \"redirect_map\": redirect_map or []}\n85\t    _emit(\"fixes\", RUN[\"fixes\"]); return {\"titles\": len(titles or []), \"redirects\": len(redirect_map or [])}\n86\t\n87\t\n88\tdef seo_recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n89\t    RUN[\"recommendations\"] = recommendations\n90\t    _emit(\"recommendations\", {\"recommendations\": recommendations}); return {\"count\": len(recommendations)}\n91\t\n92\t\n93\tdef seo_report() -> dict:\n94\t    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n95\t    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.json\")\n96\t    json.dump(_report_obj(), open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\"), indent=2)\n97\t    RUN[\"status\"] = \"done\"; _emit(\"saved\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n98\t\n99\t\n100\tdef seo_export() -> dict:\n101\t    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n102\t    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.html\")\n103\t    open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\").write(_render_html(_report_obj()))\n104\t    _emit(\"exported\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n105\t\n106\t\n107\tdef _render_html(o) -> str:\n108\t    sev = (o[\"summary\"] or {}).get(\"by_severity\", {})\n109\t    rows = \"\".join(\n110\t        f'<tr><td><span class=\"sev {i[\"severity\"].lower()}\">{i[\"severity\"]}</span></td>'\n111\t        f'<td>{i[\"type\"]}</td><td>{i[\"count\"]}</td>'\n112\t        f'<td>{(i.get(\"explanation\") or \"\")}</td></tr>'\n113\t        for i in sorted(o[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3)))\n114\t    recs = \"\".join(f\"<li>{r}</li>\" for r in o.get(\"recommendations\", []))\n115\t    return f\"\"\"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n116\t<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1\"><title>SEO Audit — {o['site']}</title>\n117\t<style>body{{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;background:#1a1a1f;color:#f8f7f4;margin:0;padding:40px;line-height:1.5}}\n118\t.wrap{{max-width:860px;margin:0 auto}}h1{{font-size:28px;margin:0 0 4px}}.sub{{color:#c8c5be;margin-bottom:24px}}\n119\t.card{{background:#242428;border:1px solid #3a3a42;border-radius:14px;padding:22px;margin-bottom:18px}}\n120\t.k{{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap}}.k div{{font-size:13px;color:#c8c5be}}.k b{{display:block;font-size:30px;color:#f8f7f4}}\n121\ttable{{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}}th,td{{text-align:left;padding:9px 10px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a42}}\n122\tth{{font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#c8c5be}}\n123\t.sev{{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:999px}}.sev.high{{background:#FF0000;color:#fff}}\n124\t.sev.medium{{background:#e2b53e;color:#1a1a1f}}.sev.low{{background:#3a3a42;color:#c8c5be}}\n125\tul{{padding-left:20px}}li{{margin:6px 0}}.muted{{color:#c8c5be;font-size:13px}}</style></head><body><div class=\"wrap\">\n126\t<h1>SEO Audit Report</h1><div class=\"sub\">{o['site']} · {o['urls_crawled']} URLs crawled</div>\n127\t<div class=\"card k\"><div><b>{(o['summary'] or {}).get('total_issues',0)}</b>total issues</div>\n128\t<div><b style=\"color:#FF0000\">{sev.get('High',0)}</b>high</div><div><b style=\"color:#e2b53e\">{sev.get('Medium',0)}</b>medium</div>\n129\t<div><b>{sev.get('Low',0)}</b>low</div></div>\n130\t<div class=\"card\"><h3>Issues (prioritized)</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Severity</th><th>Issue</th><th>URLs</th><th>What it means</th></tr></thead>\n131\t<tbody>{rows or '<tr><td colspan=4 class=muted>No issues detected.</td></tr>'}</tbody></table></div>\n132\t<div class=\"card\"><h3>Recommendations</h3><ul>{recs or '<li class=muted>None generated.</li>'}</ul></div>\n133\t<p class=\"muted\">Generated by SEO Command Center · model {o.get('run_meta',{}).get('model','')}</p></div></body></html>\"\"\"\n134\t\n135\t\n136\t# ----- dashboard HTTP host -----\n137\tclass H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n138\t    def log_message(self, *a): pass\n139\t    def _send(self, code, body, ctype=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"):\n140\t        self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)\n141\t        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n142\t        self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)\n143\t    def do_GET(self):\n144\t        if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):\n145\t            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")\n146\t            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"no dashboard\")\n147\t        elif self.path == \"/app.js\":\n148\t            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"app.js\")\n149\t            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"\", \"application/javascript\")\n150\t        elif self.path == \"/state\":\n151\t            self._send(200, json.dumps({k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}), \"application/json\")\n152\t        elif self.path == \"/events\":\n153\t            self.send_response(200); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n154\t            self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n155\t            q = queue.Queue()\n156\t            with _lock: _subs.append(q)\n157\t            try:\n158\t                snap = {k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}\n159\t                self.wfile.write(f\"data: {json.dumps({'event':'snapshot','data':snap})}\\n\\n\".encode()); self.wfile.flush()\n160\t                while True:\n161\t                    try: self.wfile.write(f\"data: {q.get(timeout=15)}\\n\\n\".encode())\n162\t                    except queue.Empty: self.wfile.write(b\": ping\\n\\n\")\n163\t                    self.wfile.flush()\n164\t            except Exception: pass\n165\t            finally:\n166\t                with _lock:\n167\t                    if q in _subs: _subs.remove(q)\n168\t        else: self._send(404, \"not found\")\n169\t\n170\t\n171\tdef start_dashboard(port=PORT):\n172\t    httpd = ThreadingHTTPServer((\"127.0.0.1\", port), H)\n173\t    threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()\n174\t    return httpd\n175\t\n176\t\n177\tdef _run_mcp():\n178\t    try:\n179\t        from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP\n180\t    except Exception:\n181\t        print(f\"[seo] MCP SDK not found. Dashboard only at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n182\t        while True: time.sleep(3600)\n183\t    mcp = FastMCP(\"seo-command-center\")\n184\t\n185\t    @mcp.tool()\n186\t    def load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n187\t        \"\"\"Load a Screaming Frog export directory (expects internal_all.csv).\"\"\"\n188\t        return seo_load(export_dir)\n189\t\n190\t    @mcp.tool()\n191\t    def detect_issues() -> dict:\n192\t        \"\"\"Run the SEO rulebook detectors over the loaded crawl.\"\"\"\n193\t        return seo_detect()\n194\t\n195\t    @mcp.tool()\n196\t    def set_fixes(titles: list = None, redirect_map: list = None) -> dict:\n197\t        \"\"\"Attach the model-written title rewrites and the redirect map.\"\"\"\n198\t        return seo_set_fixes(titles, redirect_map)\n199\t\n200\t    @mcp.tool()\n201\t    def recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n202\t        \"\"\"Attach the prioritized recommendations.\"\"\"\n203\t        return seo_recommend(recommendations)\n204\t\n205\t    @mcp.tool()\n206\t    def write_report() -> dict:\n207\t        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.json.\"\"\"\n208\t        return seo_report()\n209\t\n210\t    @mcp.tool()\n211\t    def export_report() -> dict:\n212\t        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.html (the client deliverable).\"\"\"\n213\t        return seo_export()\n214\t\n215\t    mcp.run()\n216\t\n217\t\n218\tif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n219\t    start_dashboard()\n220\t    print(f\"[seo] dashboard live at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n221\t    _run_mcp()\n222\t"}]},"uuid":"568b74a0-afc2-4dee-acf9-1cfeb1e39b59","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:16:30.087Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py","content":"\"\"\"\nserver.py — local MCP server + live dashboard host (one process, two faces).\n\n  1. MCP tools over stdio  -> Claude Code calls: seo_load, seo_detect, seo_report, seo_export\n  2. HTTP + SSE on localhost:7700 -> the live cockpit that fills as issues are found.\n\nSTARTER: works end to end out of the box. Extend the detectors (seo/detector.py) and\nthe fixes (the model-driven title rewriting / redirect map) during the Sprint.\n\nNeeds the MCP SDK to expose tools to Claude (`pip install mcp`); without it the dashboard\nstill runs so you can use run.py. Standard library otherwise.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport json, os, queue, threading, time\nfrom http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer\n\nHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\nROOT = os.path.dirname(HERE)\nDASH_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"dashboard\")\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"outputs\")\nPORT = int(os.environ.get(\"SEO_PORT\", \"7700\"))\nMODEL = os.environ.get(\"RADAR_MODEL\", \"qwen3.5:9b\")\n\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, ROOT)\nfrom seo import detector  # noqa: E402\n\nRUN = {\"site\": None, \"urls\": 0, \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None, \"status\": \"idle\"}\n_subs: list[queue.Queue] = []\n_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n\ndef _emit(event, data):\n    payload = json.dumps({\"event\": event, \"data\": data})\n    with _lock:\n        for q in list(_subs):\n            try: q.put_nowait(payload)\n            except Exception: pass\n\n\n# ----- pipeline tools (importable by run.py without MCP) -----\ndef seo_load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n    rows = detector.load_rows(export_dir)\n    RUN.update({\"rows\": rows, \"urls\": len(rows), \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None,\n                \"site\": _guess_site(rows), \"status\": \"running\"})\n    _emit(\"loaded\", {\"site\": RUN[\"site\"], \"urls\": len(rows)})\n    return {\"urls\": len(rows), \"site\": RUN[\"site\"]}\n\n\ndef _guess_site(rows):\n    if not rows: return \"unknown\"\n    addr = rows[0].get(\"Address\", \"\")\n    try:\n        from urllib.parse import urlparse\n        return urlparse(addr).netloc or \"unknown\"\n    except Exception:\n        return \"unknown\"\n\n\ndef seo_detect() -> dict:\n    issues = detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n    RUN[\"issues\"] = issues\n    RUN[\"summary\"] = detector.summarize(issues)\n    for i in issues:\n        _emit(\"issue\", i)\n    _emit(\"summary\", RUN[\"summary\"])\n    return {\"detected\": len(issues), \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"]}\n\n\ndef _report_obj() -> dict:\n    return {\n        \"site\": RUN[\"site\"],\n        \"urls_crawled\": RUN[\"urls\"],\n        \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"] or {\"total_issues\": 0, \"by_severity\": {}},\n        \"issues\": RUN[\"issues\"],\n        \"fixes\": RUN.get(\"fixes\", {\"titles\": [], \"redirect_map\": []}),\n        \"recommendations\": RUN.get(\"recommendations\", []),\n        \"run_meta\": {\"model\": MODEL, \"model_calls\": RUN.get(\"model_calls\", 0),\n                     \"duration_sec\": RUN.get(\"duration_sec\", 0)},\n    }\n\n\ndef seo_set_fixes(titles=None, redirect_map=None) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"fixes\"] = {\"titles\": titles or [], \"redirect_map\": redirect_map or []}\n    _emit(\"fixes\", RUN[\"fixes\"]); return {\"titles\": len(titles or []), \"redirects\": len(redirect_map or [])}\n\n\ndef seo_recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"recommendations\"] = recommendations\n    _emit(\"recommendations\", {\"recommendations\": recommendations}); return {\"count\": len(recommendations)}\n\n\ndef seo_report() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.json\")\n    json.dump(_report_obj(), open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\"), indent=2)\n    RUN[\"status\"] = \"done\"; _emit(\"saved\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef seo_export() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.html\")\n    open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\").write(_render_html(_report_obj()))\n    _emit(\"exported\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef _render_html(o) -> str:\n    sev = (o[\"summary\"] or {}).get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    rows = \"\".join(\n        f'<tr><td><span class=\"sev {i[\"severity\"].lower()}\">{i[\"severity\"]}</span></td>'\n        f'<td>{i[\"type\"]}</td><td>{i[\"count\"]}</td>'\n        f'<td>{(i.get(\"explanation\") or \"\")}</td></tr>'\n        for i in sorted(o[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3)))\n    recs = \"\".join(f\"<li>{r}</li>\" for r in o.get(\"recommendations\", []))\n    return f\"\"\"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1\"><title>SEO Audit — {o['site']}</title>\n<style>body{{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;background:#1a1a1f;color:#f8f7f4;margin:0;padding:40px;line-height:1.5}}\n.wrap{{max-width:860px;margin:0 auto}}h1{{font-size:28px;margin:0 0 4px}}.sub{{color:#c8c5be;margin-bottom:24px}}\n.card{{background:#242428;border:1px solid #3a3a42;border-radius:14px;padding:22px;margin-bottom:18px}}\n.k{{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap}}.k div{{font-size:13px;color:#c8c5be}}.k b{{display:block;font-size:30px;color:#f8f7f4}}\ntable{{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}}th,td{{text-align:left;padding:9px 10px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a42}}\nth{{font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#c8c5be}}\n.sev{{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:999px}}.sev.high{{background:#FF0000;color:#fff}}\n.sev.medium{{background:#e2b53e;color:#1a1a1f}}.sev.low{{background:#3a3a42;color:#c8c5be}}\nul{{padding-left:20px}}li{{margin:6px 0}}.muted{{color:#c8c5be;font-size:13px}}</style></head><body><div class=\"wrap\">\n<h1>SEO Audit Report</h1><div class=\"sub\">{o['site']} · {o['urls_crawled']} URLs crawled</div>\n<div class=\"card k\"><div><b>{(o['summary'] or {}).get('total_issues',0)}</b>total issues</div>\n<div><b style=\"color:#FF0000\">{sev.get('High',0)}</b>high</div><div><b style=\"color:#e2b53e\">{sev.get('Medium',0)}</b>medium</div>\n<div><b>{sev.get('Low',0)}</b>low</div></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Issues (prioritized)</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Severity</th><th>Issue</th><th>URLs</th><th>What it means</th></tr></thead>\n<tbody>{rows or '<tr><td colspan=4 class=muted>No issues detected.</td></tr>'}</tbody></table></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Recommendations</h3><ul>{recs or '<li class=muted>None generated.</li>'}</ul></div>\n<p class=\"muted\">Generated by SEO Command Center · model {o.get('run_meta',{}).get('model','')}</p></div></body></html>\"\"\"\n\n\n# ----- dashboard HTTP host -----\nclass H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n    def log_message(self, *a): pass\n    def _send(self, code, body, ctype=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"):\n        self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)\n    def do_GET(self):\n        if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"no dashboard\")\n        elif self.path == \"/app.js\":\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"app.js\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"\", \"application/javascript\")\n        elif self.path == \"/state\":\n            self._send(200, json.dumps({k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}), \"application/json\")\n        elif self.path == \"/events\":\n            self.send_response(200); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n            self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n            q = queue.Queue()\n            with _lock: _subs.append(q)\n            try:\n                snap = {k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}\n                self.wfile.write(f\"data: {json.dumps({'event':'snapshot','data':snap})}\\n\\n\".encode()); self.wfile.flush()\n                while True:\n                    try: self.wfile.write(f\"data: {q.get(timeout=15)}\\n\\n\".encode())\n                    except queue.Empty: self.wfile.write(b\": ping\\n\\n\")\n                    self.wfile.flush()\n            except Exception: pass\n            finally:\n                with _lock:\n                    if q in _subs: _subs.remove(q)\n        else: self._send(404, \"not found\")\n\n\ndef start_dashboard(port=PORT):\n    httpd = ThreadingHTTPServer((\"127.0.0.1\", port), H)\n    threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()\n    return httpd\n\n\ndef _run_mcp():\n    try:\n        from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP\n    except Exception:\n        print(f\"[seo] MCP SDK not found. Dashboard only at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n        while True: time.sleep(3600)\n    mcp = FastMCP(\"seo-command-center\")\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Load a Screaming Frog export directory (expects internal_all.csv).\"\"\"\n        return seo_load(export_dir)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def detect_issues() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Run the SEO rulebook detectors over the loaded crawl.\"\"\"\n        return seo_detect()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def set_fixes(titles: list = None, redirect_map: list = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the model-written title rewrites and the redirect map.\"\"\"\n        return seo_set_fixes(titles, redirect_map)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the prioritized recommendations.\"\"\"\n        return seo_recommend(recommendations)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def write_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.json.\"\"\"\n        return seo_report()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def export_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.html (the client deliverable).\"\"\"\n        return seo_export()\n\n    mcp.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    start_dashboard()\n    print(f\"[seo] dashboard live at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n    _run_mcp()\n","numLines":222,"startLine":1,"totalLines":222}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"156593af-092e-4c3b-9c16-810affa9ed70","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

What is already implemented\n*   **Core Detection Engine (`seo/detector.py`):** Basic infrastructure to load `internal_all.csv` and deterministic detectors for:\n    *   Missing, duplicate, and over-length titles.\n    *   Broken links (4xx) and server errors (5xx).\n    *   Redirects (3xx) and orphan pages.\n*   **MCP Server & Dashboard (`mcp/server.py`):** \n    *   A fully functional MCP server with tools for loading, detecting, fixing, and reporting.\n    *   A live dashboard hosted at `localhost:7700` using SSE (Server-Sent Events) for real-time updates.\n    *   An HTML report generator that outputs to `outputs/report.html`.\n*   **Orchestration Framework:** The structure for the audit pipeline is in place, including agent definitions (`agents/*.md`), a skill definition (`skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md`), and an end-to-end test script (`run.py`).\n\n### 2. What is missing\n*   **Full Rulebook Implementation:** A significant number of deterministic detectors are missing from `seo/detector.py`, including:\n    *   Titles too short, missing/duplicate/too-long meta descriptions.\n    *   Missing or duplicate H1 tags.\n    *   Redirect chains, thin content, slow pages, and non-indexable but linked pages.\n*   **Crucial Reference Files:** Both `rulebook.md` (the source of truth for SEO rules) and `report.schema.json` (the required format for `report.json`) are missing from the repository.\n*   **Model-Driven Logic:** While the infrastructure exists, the actual \"judgment\" logic (rewriting titles/metas and prioritizing issues) needs to be implemented and refined within the agent prompts.\n\n### 3. Files you need to work on\n*   **`seo/detector.py`**: This is your primary focus for increasing the \"score\" by completing the rulebook implementation.\n*   **`agents/auditor.md`, `agents/fixer.md`, `agents/reporter.md`**: These need to be refined to handle the judgment-based tasks (prioritization and rewriting).\n*   **`skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md`**: Use this to improve how the sub-agents are orchestrated.\n*   **`outputs/report.json` & `report.schema.json`**: You will need to ensure the JSON output strictly adheres to the schema (which needs to be located or created)."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":62329,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":642,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"6513886a-1058-4537-9af0-12cc227f8683","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:17:21.177Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"14d0894f-4f77-46ba-99a6-4ac44bf58c55","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_d4c03401ade0291773ee584d","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Based on the code in `seo/detector.py`, here is the breakdown of the detectors:\n\n### ✅ Existing Detectors\nThese are already implemented in `detect()`:\n1.  **`missing_title`**: Detects indexable pages with no title tag.\n2.  **`duplicate_title`**: Detects indexable pages sharing an identical title.\n3.  **`title_too_long`**: Detects titles that exceed 561 pixels or 60 characters.\n4.  **`broken_link`**: Detects URLs returning 4xx client errors.\n5.  **`server_error`**: Detects URLs returning 5xx server errors.\n6.  **`redirect`**: Detects URLs returning 3xx redirects.\n7.  **`orphan_page`**: Detects indexable pages with zero internal in-links.\n\n### ❌ Missing Detectors\nAccording to the `TODO` list in `seo/detector.py` (which references the rulebook), the following are still missing:\n1.  **`title_too_short`**: Titles that are under the recommended length.\n2.  **`missing_meta_description`**: Pages without a meta description tag.\n3.  **`duplicate_meta_description`**: Pages sharing an identical meta description.\n4.  **`meta_description_too_long`**: Meta descriptions exceeding recommended length.\n5.  **`missing_h1`**: Pages missing an H1 tag.\n6.  **`duplicate_h1`**: Pages with more than one H1 tag (or identical H1s across pages).\n7.  **`redirect_chain`**: Detecting sequences of redirects before hitting the final destination.\n8.  **`thin_content`**: Pages with very low word count or content density.\n9.  **`non_indexable_but_linked`**: Pages marked \"noindex\" that still have internal links pointing to them.\n10. **`slow_page`**: Pages with high load times (based on the Screaming Frog response time column)."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":61907,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":457,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"9918eee9-ea34-4209-b70c-a28dff73ad4c","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:19:32.662Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter I will use standard SEO industry thresholds:\n- `title_too_short`: < 30 characters.\n- `missing_meta_description`: Empty.\n- `duplicate_meta_description`: Exact matches.\n- `meta_description_too_long`: > 160 characters.\n\nWait, looking at the existing code in `seo/detector.py`:\n`title_too_long` is `_int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60`.\n\nFor `title_too_short`, a common threshold is 30 chars.\nFor `meta_description`:\nScreaming Frog columns for meta description are usually \"Meta Description 1\" and \"Meta Description 1 Length\".\n\nLet's refine the plan:\n1. `title_too_short`: Use `Title 1 Length` < 30.\n2. `missing_meta_description`: `Meta Description 1` is empty.\n3. `duplicate_meta_description`: `Meta Description 1` are identical.\n4. `meta_description_too_long`: `Meta Description 1 Length` > 160.\n\nI'll implement these using the `add` helper function in `detect()`.\n\nImplementation details:\n- `title_too_short`: `[r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30]` (exclude missing titles).\n- `missing_meta_description`: `[r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()]`.\n- `duplicate_meta_description`: Same logic as `duplicate_title` but using `Meta Description 1`.\n- `meta_description_too_long`: `[r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160]`.\n\nLet's check the file content again to place them correctly.\nThe `TODO` is on line 93. duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")","new_string":"    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Meta Descriptions ---\n    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")\n\n    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if m:\n            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":63030,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":1359,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"e8a4296b-27ea-44e5-b086-c936101e6c58","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:25:26.426Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

--- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")","newString":"    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],\n        \"Titles that are too short to be effective.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Meta Descriptions ---\n    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")\n\n    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if m:\n            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n","originalFile":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n\nSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":59,"oldLines":6,"newStart":59,"newLines":10,"lines":["         [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],","         \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")"," ","+    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",","+        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],","+        \"Titles that are too short to be effective.\")","+","     # duplicate titles (indexable only)","     by_title = defaultdict(list)","     for r in idx200:"]},{"oldStart":73,"oldLines":6,"newStart":77,"newLines":24,"lines":["          if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],","         \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")"," ","+    # --- Meta Descriptions ---","+    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",","+        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],","+        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")","+","+    by_meta = defaultdict(list)","+    for r in idx200:","+        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()","+        if m:","+            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])","+    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]","+    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")","+","+    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",","+        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160],","+        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")","+","+","     # {"parentUuid":"4a0b2ef1-2fcc-47db-96f8-d8a8f389c872","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_0861fc06544db48669a3f14e","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I have implemented the requested detectors in `seo/detector.py`.\n\n**Added Detectors:**\n1.  **`title_too_short`**: Identifies indexable pages with titles shorter than 30 characters (Severity: Low).\n2.  **`missing_meta_description`**: Identifies indexable pages that have no meta description (Severity: Medium).\n3.  **`duplicate_meta_description`**: Identifies indexable pages that share the exact same meta description (Severity: Medium).\n4.  **`meta_description_too_long`**: Identifies indexable pages with meta descriptions exceeding 160 characters (Severity: Low).\n\n**Implementation Details:**\n*   **Patterns:** I followed the established pattern of using the `add()` helper and the `idx200` (indexable 200 OK) filtered list.\n*   **Thresholds:** Used industry-standard thresholds (30 chars for short titles, 160 chars for long descriptions) since the specific `rulebook.md` was not found in the repository.\n*   **Logic:** For duplicates, I used a `defaultdict` to group URLs by their meta description, matching the existing implementation for duplicate titles."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":64451,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":264,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"fda05783-c693-4c89-89fd-de0b9fbd5062","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:25:48.618Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"ff7a4a4a-063b-4cb9-addb-c059fab27da5","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"selected_lines_in_ide","ideName":"Visual Studio Code","lineStart":0,"lineEnd":21,"filename":"d:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json","content":"{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"SessionStart\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"UserPromptSubmit\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=UserPromptSubmit bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"PreToolUse\": [\n      { \"matcher\": \".*\", \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=PreToolUse bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"PostToolUse\": [\n      { \"matcher\": \".*\", \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=PostToolUse bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"SubagentStop\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SubagentStop bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"Stop\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=Stop bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n","displayPath":".claude\\settings.json"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"4f6e179f-4de5-45c2-952b-c5ab067c2b53","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:27:06.195Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"9b23d29a-7117-448d-865c-e7d5ce34aeed","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"cdd1a008-2feb-4254-915b-45d2a8f63590","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_pr92p7a9","type":"tool_result","content":"The file D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"ab46acea-9ef6-40fe-b905-7b6939c785f8","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:30:42.492Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","oldString":"    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")","newString":"    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")","originalFile":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n\nSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],\n        \"Titles that are too short to be effective.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Meta Descriptions ---\n    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")\n\n    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if m:\n            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":91,"oldLines":7,"newStart":91,"newLines":7,"lines":["     add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")"," ","     add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",","-        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 160],","+        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],","         \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")"," "," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9b23d29a-7117-448d-865c-e7d5ce34aeed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

(file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"98d8a0ca-6376-4b22-8c8c-09e88eaf9e4c","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:45:11.799Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","oldString":"    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")","newString":"    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n\n    # --- H1 Tags ---\n    add(\"missing_h1\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    by_h1 = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        h = (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if h:\n            by_h1[h].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_h1 = [u for urls in by_h1.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"High\", dup_h1, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n","originalFile":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n\nSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],\n        \"Titles that are too short to be effective.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Meta Descriptions ---\n    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")\n\n    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if m:\n            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":94,"oldLines":7,"newStart":94,"newLines":21,"lines":["         [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],","         \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")"," ","+    # --- H1 Tags ---","+    add(\"missing_h1\", \"High\",","+        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],","+        \"Indexable pages missing an H1 tag.\")"," ","+    by_h1 = defaultdict(list)","+    for r in idx200:","+        h = (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()","+        if h:","+            by_h1[h].append(r[\"Address\"])","+    dup_h1 = [u for urls in by_h1.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]","+    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"High\", dup_h1, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")","+","+","+","     # --- Response codes ---","     add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",","         [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f0b255e4-090d-4d71-a9ea-38633e228116","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"98d8a0ca-6376-4b22-8c8c-09e88eaf9e4c","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_1599c8e62fe738ba341c6c71","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I have implemented the H1 detectors in `seo/detector.py`.\n\n**Implementation Details:**\n*   **`missing_h1`**: I added a check that identifies indexable 200-OK pages where the `H1-1` column is empty or contains only whitespace. I assigned this a **High** severity.\n*   **`duplicate_h1`**: I implemented a grouping mechanism using a `defaultdict` to find all non-empty H1 values that appear on more than one indexable page. All affected URLs are flagged. I assigned this a **High** severity to match the priority of duplicate titles.\n*   **Consistency**: Both detectors use the `idx200` list to ensure we only audit pages that are actually indexable and returning a success code, following the pattern used for titles and meta descriptions."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":66309,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":179,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"4ac6f90f-3e19-48d1-850e-f3f33aea2f63","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:45:46.651Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"2690733d-8e31-4a5f-a27b-cc0eaff26288","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"selected_lines_in_ide","ideName":"Visual Studio Code","lineStart":0,"lineEnd":21,"filename":"d:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json","content":"{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"SessionStart\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"UserPromptSubmit\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=UserPromptSubmit bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"PreToolUse\": [\n      { \"matcher\": \".*\", \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=PreToolUse bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"PostToolUse\": [\n      { \"matcher\": \".*\", \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=PostToolUse bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"SubagentStop\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SubagentStop bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"Stop\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=Stop bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n","displayPath":".claude\\settings.json"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"53d9d26c-e964-4766-8083-531370e8ca75","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:52:39.993Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"53d9d26c-e964-4766-8083-531370e8ca75","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_989a1a8c1d3f02cca4789799","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_d810vl28","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\rulebook.md"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":66427,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":39,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"fef519a6-fb4a-4ce6-9756-b51045a46265","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:52:55.422Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"fef519a6-fb4a-4ce6-9756-b51045a46265","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"8356fd78-2b53-4f53-8cf8-cad6845e360a","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d810vl28","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Forge Sprint 01 — SEO Detection Rulebook\n2\t\n3\tDetect these issues from `internal_all.csv`. Each is a deterministic rule over the\n4\tcolumns, so you need no SEO background. The grader's ground truth uses these same\n5\trules — match them precisely.\n6\t\n7\t## Pre-filters (apply before title/meta/H1 checks)\n8\t- Only consider rows where `Content Type` contains `text/html`.\n9\t- For \"duplicate\" checks, only compare **Indexable** 200 pages.\n10\t- A page is \"indexable\" when `Indexability` = `Indexable`.\n11\t\n12\t## Rules\n13\t\n14\t| type (use this exact string) | Rule | Severity |\n15\t|---|---|---|\n16\t| `missing_title` | `Title 1` empty, indexable 200 page | High |\n17\t| `duplicate_title` | same `Title 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | High |\n18\t| `title_too_long` | `Title 1 Pixel Width` > 561 OR `Title 1 Length` > 60 | Medium |\n19\t| `title_too_short` | `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty) | Low |\n20\t| `missing_meta_description` | `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page | Medium |\n21\t| `duplicate_meta_description` | same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Medium |\n22\t| `meta_description_too_long` | `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155 | Low |\n23\t| `missing_h1` | `H1-1` empty on a 200 page | Medium |\n24\t| `duplicate_h1` | same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Low |\n25\t| `broken_link` | `Status Code` in 400–499 | High |\n26\t| `server_error` | `Status Code` in 500–599 | High |\n27\t| `redirect` | `Status Code` in 300–399 | Medium |\n28\t| `redirect_chain` | a redirect whose `Redirect URL` is itself a redirecting URL | High |\n29\t| `thin_content` | `Word Count` < 200 on an indexable page | Low |\n30\t| `orphan_page` | `Inlinks` = 0 on an indexable 200 page | Medium |\n31\t| `non_indexable_but_linked` | `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0 | Medium |\n32\t| `slow_page` | `Response Time` > 1.0 | Low |\n33\t\n34\t## Notes\n35\t- `redirect_chain`: build a map of {Address -> Redirect URL} for all 3xx rows, then a\n36\t  chain exists when a Redirect URL target is also a key in that map. A loop is a chain\n37\t  that returns to an earlier URL.\n38\t- Count = number of affected URLs. `affected_urls` = the list of those URLs.\n39\t- Severity strings must be exactly `High`, `Medium`, or `Low`.\n40\t- The hidden test export uses the SAME rules on a different site. Do not hard-code URLs.\n41\t"}]},"uuid":"1875c128-4afb-4416-addd-fa12da525d1f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:53:02.192Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\rulebook.md","content":"# Forge Sprint 01 — SEO Detection Rulebook\n\nDetect these issues from `internal_all.csv`. Each is a deterministic rule over the\ncolumns, so you need no SEO background. The grader's ground truth uses these same\nrules — match them precisely.\n\n## Pre-filters (apply before title/meta/H1 checks)\n- Only consider rows where `Content Type` contains `text/html`.\n- For \"duplicate\" checks, only compare **Indexable** 200 pages.\n- A page is \"indexable\" when `Indexability` = `Indexable`.\n\n## Rules\n\n| type (use this exact string) | Rule | Severity |\n|---|---|---|\n| `missing_title` | `Title 1` empty, indexable 200 page | High |\n| `duplicate_title` | same `Title 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | High |\n| `title_too_long` | `Title 1 Pixel Width` > 561 OR `Title 1 Length` > 60 | Medium |\n| `title_too_short` | `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty) | Low |\n| `missing_meta_description` | `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page | Medium |\n| `duplicate_meta_description` | same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Medium |\n| `meta_description_too_long` | `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155 | Low |\n| `missing_h1` | `H1-1` empty on a 200 page | Medium |\n| `duplicate_h1` | same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Low |\n| `broken_link` | `Status Code` in 400–499 | High |\n| `server_error` | `Status Code` in 500–599 | High |\n| `redirect` | `Status Code` in 300–399 | Medium |\n| `redirect_chain` | a redirect whose `Redirect URL` is itself a redirecting URL | High |\n| `thin_content` | `Word Count` < 200 on an indexable page | Low |\n| `orphan_page` | `Inlinks` = 0 on an indexable 200 page | Medium |\n| `non_indexable_but_linked` | `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0 | Medium |\n| `slow_page` | `Response Time` > 1.0 | Low |\n\n## Notes\n- `redirect_chain`: build a map of {Address -> Redirect URL} for all 3xx rows, then a\n  chain exists when a Redirect URL target is also a key in that map. A loop is a chain\n  that returns to an earlier URL.\n- Count = number of affected URLs. `affected_urls` = the list of those URLs.\n- Severity strings must be exactly `High`, `Medium`, or `Low`.\n- The hidden test export uses the SAME rules on a different site. Do not hard-code URLs.\n","numLines":41,"startLine":1,"totalLines":41}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"fef519a6-fb4a-4ce6-9756-b51045a46265","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],\n        \"Titles that are too short to be effective.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Meta Descriptions ---\n    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")\n\n    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if m:\n            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n\n    # --- H1 Tags ---\n    add(\"missing_h1\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    by_h1 = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        h = (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if h:\n            by_h1[h].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_h1 = [u for urls in by_h1.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"High\", dup_h1, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n\n\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":95,"oldLines":7,"newStart":95,"newLines":7,"lines":["         \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")"," ","     # --- H1 Tags ---","-    add(\"missing_h1\", \"High\",","+    add(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\",","         [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],","         \"Indexable pages missing an H1 tag.\")"," "]},{"oldStart":105,"oldLines":7,"newStart":105,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if h:","             by_h1[h].append(r[\"Address\"])","     dup_h1 = [u for urls in by_h1.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]","-    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"High\", dup_h1, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")","+    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")"," "," "," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"cc2e4440-7ab6-4edb-a23e-40413ecdc3ed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
