{"parentUuid":"f6d3884d-bfe8-46c7-bc17-98b114d39ccf","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py","content":"\"\"\"\nserver.py — local MCP server + live dashboard host (one process, two faces).\n\n  1. MCP tools over stdio  -> Claude Code calls: seo_load, seo_detect, seo_report, seo_export\n  2. HTTP + SSE on localhost:7700 -> the live cockpit that fills as issues are found.\n\nSTARTER: works end to end out of the box. Extend the detectors (seo/detector.py) and\nthe fixes (the model-driven title rewriting / redirect map) during the Sprint.\n\nNeeds the MCP SDK to expose tools to Claude (`pip install mcp`); without it the dashboard\nstill runs so you can use run.py. Standard library otherwise.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport json, os, queue, threading, time\nfrom http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer\n\nHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\nROOT = os.path.dirname(HERE)\nDASH_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"dashboard\")\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"outputs\")\nPORT = int(os.environ.get(\"SEO_PORT\", \"7700\"))\nMODEL = os.environ.get(\"RADAR_MODEL\", \"qwen3.5:9b\")\n\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, ROOT)\nfrom seo import detector  # noqa: E402\n\nRUN = {\"site\": None, \"urls\": 0, \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None, \"status\": \"idle\"}\n_subs: list[queue.Queue] = []\n_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n\ndef _emit(event, data):\n    payload = json.dumps({\"event\": event, \"data\": data})\n    with _lock:\n        for q in list(_subs):\n            try: q.put_nowait(payload)\n            except Exception: pass\n\n\n# ----- pipeline tools (importable by run.py without MCP) -----\ndef seo_load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n    rows = detector.load_rows(export_dir)\n    RUN.update({\"rows\": rows, \"urls\": len(rows), \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None,\n                \"site\": _guess_site(rows), \"status\": \"running\"})\n    _emit(\"loaded\", {\"site\": RUN[\"site\"], \"urls\": len(rows)})\n    return {\"urls\": len(rows), \"site\": RUN[\"site\"]}\n\n\ndef _guess_site(rows):\n    if not rows: return \"unknown\"\n    addr = rows[0].get(\"Address\", \"\")\n    try:\n        from urllib.parse import urlparse\n        return urlparse(addr).netloc or \"unknown\"\n    except Exception:\n        return \"unknown\"\n\n\ndef seo_detect() -> dict:\n    issues = detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n    RUN[\"issues\"] = issues\n    RUN[\"summary\"] = detector.summarize(issues)\n    for i in issues:\n        _emit(\"issue\", i)\n    _emit(\"summary\", RUN[\"summary\"])\n    return {\"detected\": len(issues), \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"]}\n\n\ndef _report_obj() -> dict:\n    return {\n        \"site\": RUN[\"site\"],\n        \"urls_crawled\": RUN[\"urls\"],\n        \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"] or {\"total_issues\": 0, \"by_severity\": {}},\n        \"issues\": RUN[\"issues\"],\n        \"fixes\": RUN.get(\"fixes\", {\"titles\": [], \"redirect_map\": []}),\n        \"recommendations\": RUN.get(\"recommendations\", []),\n        \"run_meta\": {\n            \"model\": MODEL,\n            \"model_calls\": RUN.get(\"model_calls\", 0),\n            \"duration_sec\": RUN.get(\"duration_sec\", 0),\n        },\n    }\n\n\ndef seo_set_fixes(titles=None, redirect_map=None) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"fixes\"] = {\"titles\": titles or [], \"redirect_map\": redirect_map or []}\n    _emit(\"fixes\", RUN[\"fixes\"]); return {\"titles\": len(titles or []), \"redirects\": len(redirect_map or [])}\n\n\ndef seo_recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"recommendations\"] = recommendations\n    _emit(\"recommendations\", {\"recommendations\": recommendations}); return {\"count\": len(recommendations)}\n\n\ndef seo_report() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.json\")\n    json.dump(_report_obj(), open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\"), indent=2)\n    RUN[\"status\"] = \"done\"; _emit(\"saved\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef seo_export() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.html\")\n    open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\").write(_render_html(_report_obj()))\n    _emit(\"exported\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef _render_html(o) -> str:\n    sev = (o[\"summary\"] or {}).get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    rows = \"\".join(\n        f'<tr><td><span class=\"sev {i[\"severity\"].lower()}\">{i[\"severity\"]}</span></td>'\n        f'<td>{i[\"type\"]}</td><td>{i[\"count\"]}</td>'\n        f'<td>{(i.get(\"explanation\") or \"\")}</td></tr>'\n        for i in sorted(o[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3)))\n    recs = \"\".join(f\"<li>{r}</li>\" for r in o.get(\"recommendations\", []))\n    return f\"\"\"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1\"><title>SEO Audit — {o['site']}</title>\n<style>body{{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;background:#1a1a1f;color:#f8f7f4;margin:0;padding:40px;line-height:1.5}}\n.wrap{{max-width:860px;margin:0 auto}}h1{{font-size:28px;margin:0 0 4px}}.sub{{color:#c8c5be;margin-bottom:24px}}\n.card{{background:#242428;border:1px solid #3a3a42;border-radius:14px;padding:22px;margin-bottom:18px}}\n.k{{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap}}.k div{{font-size:13px;color:#c8c5be}}.k b{{display:block;font-size:30px;color:#f8f7f4}}\ntable{{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}}th,td{{text-align:left;padding:9px 10px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a42}}\nth{{font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#c8c5be}}\n.sev{{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:999px}}.sev.high{{background:#FF0000;color:#fff}}\n.sev.medium{{background:#e2b53e;color:#1a1a1f}}.sev.low{{background:#3a3a42;color:#c8c5be}}\nul{{padding-left:20px}}li{{margin:6px 0}}.muted{{color:#c8c5be;font-size:13px}}</style></head><body><div class=\"wrap\">\n<h1>SEO Audit Report</h1><div class=\"sub\">{o['site']} · {o['urls_crawled']} URLs crawled</div>\n<div class=\"card k\"><div><b>{(o['summary'] or {}).get('total_issues',0)}</b>total issues</div>\n<div><b style=\"color:#FF0000\">{sev.get('High',0)}</b>high</div><div><b style=\"color:#e2b53e\">{sev.get('Medium',0)}</b>medium</div>\n<div><b>{sev.get('Low',0)}</b>low</div></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Issues (prioritized)</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Severity</th><th>Issue</th><th>URLs</th><th>What it means</th></tr></thead>\n<tbody>{rows or '<tr><td colspan=4 class=muted>No issues detected.</td></tr>'}</tbody></table></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Recommendations</h3><ul>{recs or '<li class=muted>None generated.</li>'}</ul></div>\n<p class=\"muted\">Generated by SEO Command Center · model {o.get('run_meta',{}).get('model','')}</p></div></body></html>\"\"\"\n\n\n# ----- dashboard HTTP host -----\nclass H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n    def log_message(self, *a): pass\n    def _send(self, code, body, ctype=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"):\n        self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)\n    def do_GET(self):\n        if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"no dashboard\")\n        elif self.path == \"/app.js\":\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"app.js\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"\", \"application/javascript\")\n        elif self.path == \"/state\":\n            self._send(200, json.dumps({k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}), \"application/json\")\n        elif self.path == \"/events\":\n            self.send_response(200); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n            self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n            q = queue.Queue()\n            with _lock: _subs.append(q)\n            try:\n                snap = {k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}\n                self.wfile.write(f\"data: {json.dumps({'event':'snapshot','data':snap})}\\n\\n\".encode()); self.wfile.flush()\n                while True:\n                    try: self.wfile.write(f\"data: {q.get(timeout=15)}\\n\\n\".encode())\n                    except queue.Empty: self.wfile.write(b\": ping\\n\\n\")\n                    self.wfile.flush()\n            except Exception: pass\n            finally:\n                with _lock:\n                    if q in _subs: _subs.remove(q)\n        else: self._send(404, \"not found\")\n\n\ndef start_dashboard(port=PORT):\n    httpd = ThreadingHTTPServer((\"127.0.0.1\", port), H)\n    threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()\n    return httpd\n\n\ndef _run_mcp():\n    try:\n        from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP\n    except Exception:\n        print(f\"[seo] MCP SDK not found. Dashboard only at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n        while True: time.sleep(3600)\n    mcp = FastMCP(\"seo-command-center\")\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Load a Screaming Frog export directory (expects internal_all.csv).\"\"\"\n        return seo_load(export_dir)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def detect_issues() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Run the SEO rulebook detectors over the loaded crawl.\"\"\"\n        return seo_detect()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def set_fixes(titles: list = None, redirect_map: list = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the model-written title rewrites and the redirect map.\"\"\"\n        return seo_set_fixes(titles, redirect_map)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the prioritized recommendations.\"\"\"\n        return seo_recommend(recommendations)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def write_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.json.\"\"\"\n        return seo_report()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def export_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.html (the client deliverable).\"\"\"\n        return seo_export()\n\n    mcp.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    start_dashboard()\n    print(f\"[seo] dashboard live at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n    _run_mcp()\n","numLines":225,"startLine":1,"totalLines":225}},"displayPath":"mcp\\server.py"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"f3d57b9b-73c2-427f-8e83-ba05015c45a4","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:59:11.813Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"unified-wandering-whale"}
{"parentUuid":"f3d57b9b-73c2-427f-8e83-ba05015c45a4","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","content":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n\nSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if 0 < _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],\n        \"Titles that are too short to be effective.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Meta Descriptions ---\n    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing a meta description.\")\n\n    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if m:\n            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_m = [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_m, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n        \"Meta descriptions that are too long and will be truncated.\")\n\n    # --- H1 Tags ---\n    add(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    by_h1 = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        h = (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if h:\n            by_h1[h].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_h1 = [u for urls in by_h1.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n\n\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # redirect chains (Address -> Redirect URL)\n    redirect_map = {r[\"Address\"]: r.get(\"Redirect URL\", \"\")\n                    for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399}\n    chains = [addr for addr, target in redirect_map.items() if target in redirect_map]\n    add(\"redirect_chain\", \"High\", chains, \"Redirects that lead to another redirecting URL.\")\n\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    add(\"thin_content\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\" and _int(r.get(\"Word Count\")) < 200],\n        \"Indexable pages with low word count (thin content).\")\n\n    add(\"non_indexable_but_linked\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"non-indexable\" and _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) > 0],\n        \"Pages marked non-indexable that still have internal links.\")\n\n    add(\"slow_page\", \"Low\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if _float(r.get(\"Response Time\")) > 1.0],\n        \"Pages with response times exceeding 1 second.\")\n\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","numLines":176,"startLine":1,"totalLines":176}},"displayPath":"seo\\detector.py"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"0f9f71eb-cc2a-4f08-893f-a517aa4e764c","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:59:11.813Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"unified-wandering-whale"}
{"parentUuid":"0f9f71eb-cc2a-4f08-893f-a517aa4e764c","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center\\.claude\\settings.json","content":"{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"SessionStart\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"UserPromptSubmit\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=UserPromptSubmit bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"PreToolUse\": [\n      { \"matcher\": \".*\", \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=PreToolUse bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"PostToolUse\": [\n      { \"matcher\": \".*\", \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=PostToolUse bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"SubagentStop\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=SubagentStop bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ],\n    \"Stop\": [\n      { \"hooks\": [ { \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"HOOK_EVENT=Stop bash .claude/hooks/audit.sh\", \"async\": true } ] }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n","numLines":23,"startLine":1,"totalLines":23}},"displayPath":".claude\\settings.json"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"6212dc69-21a0-4ee4-b850-e045602b1e1e","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:59:11.809Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"unified-wandering-whale"}
{"parentUuid":"6212dc69-21a0-4ee4-b850-e045602b1e1e","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\rulebook.md","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\rulebook.md","content":"# Forge Sprint 01 — SEO Detection Rulebook\n\nDetect these issues from `internal_all.csv`. Each is a deterministic rule over the\ncolumns, so you need no SEO background. The grader's ground truth uses these same\nrules — match them precisely.\n\n## Pre-filters (apply before title/meta/H1 checks)\n- Only consider rows where `Content Type` contains `text/html`.\n- For \"duplicate\" checks, only compare **Indexable** 200 pages.\n- A page is \"indexable\" when `Indexability` = `Indexable`.\n\n## Rules\n\n| type (use this exact string) | Rule | Severity |\n|---|---|---|\n| `missing_title` | `Title 1` empty, indexable 200 page | High |\n| `duplicate_title` | same `Title 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | High |\n| `title_too_long` | `Title 1 Pixel Width` > 561 OR `Title 1 Length` > 60 | Medium |\n| `title_too_short` | `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty) | Low |\n| `missing_meta_description` | `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page | Medium |\n| `duplicate_meta_description` | same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Medium |\n| `meta_description_too_long` | `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155 | Low |\n| `missing_h1` | `H1-1` empty on a 200 page | Medium |\n| `duplicate_h1` | same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Low |\n| `broken_link` | `Status Code` in 400–499 | High |\n| `server_error` | `Status Code` in 500–599 | High |\n| `redirect` | `Status Code` in 300–399 | Medium |\n| `redirect_chain` | a redirect whose `Redirect URL` is itself a redirecting URL | High |\n| `thin_content` | `Word Count` < 200 on an indexable page | Low |\n| `orphan_page` | `Inlinks` = 0 on an indexable 200 page | Medium |\n| `non_indexable_but_linked` | `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0 | Medium |\n| `slow_page` | `Response Time` > 1.0 | Low |\n\n## Notes\n- `redirect_chain`: build a map of {Address -> Redirect URL} for all 3xx rows, then a\n  chain exists when a Redirect URL target is also a key in that map. A loop is a chain\n  that returns to an earlier URL.\n- Count = number of affected URLs. `affected_urls` = the list of those URLs.\n- Severity strings must be exactly `High`, `Medium`, or `Low`.\n- The hidden test export uses the SAME rules on a different site. Do not hard-code URLs.\n","numLines":41,"startLine":1,"totalLines":41}},"displayPath":"..\\rulebook.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"88505c73-8f8b-45d9-99a7-0c80b543e75d","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:59:11.809Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"unified-wandering-whale"}
{"parentUuid":"88505c73-8f8b-45d9-99a7-0c80b543e75d","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\report.schema.json","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\report.schema.json","content":"{\n  \"$schema\": \"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#\",\n  \"title\": \"Forge Sprint 01 — report.json output contract\",\n  \"type\": \"object\",\n  \"required\": [\"site\", \"urls_crawled\", \"summary\", \"issues\", \"run_meta\"],\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"site\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"urls_crawled\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n    \"summary\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"required\": [\"total_issues\", \"by_severity\"],\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"total_issues\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n        \"by_severity\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"properties\": {\n            \"High\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n            \"Medium\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n            \"Low\": { \"type\": \"integer\" }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"issues\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"required\": [\"type\", \"severity\", \"affected_urls\", \"count\"],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"type\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"severity\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"enum\": [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"] },\n          \"affected_urls\": { \"type\": \"array\", \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" } },\n          \"count\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n          \"explanation\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"fixes\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"titles\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"required\": [\"url\", \"new\"],\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"url\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"old\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"new\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        \"redirect_map\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"required\": [\"from\", \"to\"],\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"from\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"to\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"reason\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"recommendations\": { \"type\": \"array\", \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" } },\n    \"run_meta\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"required\": [\"model_calls\"],\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"model\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"model_calls\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n        \"duration_sec\": { \"type\": \"number\" }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":79,"startLine":1,"totalLines":79}},"displayPath":"..\\report.schema.json"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"0bb92127-ed8e-4c4b-85b3-86ae48397aaa","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:59:11.809Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"unified-wandering-whale"}
{"parentUuid":"0bb92127-ed8e-4c4b-85b3-86ae48397aaa","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"invoked_skills","skills":[{"name":"update-config","path":"bundled:update-config","content":"# Update Config Skill\n\nModify Claude Code configuration by updating settings.json files.\n\n## When Hooks Are Required (Not Memory)\n\nIf the user wants something to happen automatically in response to an EVENT, they need a **hook** configured in settings.json. Memory/preferences cannot trigger automated actions.\n\n**These require hooks:**\n- \"Before compacting, ask me what to preserve\" → PreCompact hook\n- \"After writing files, run prettier\" → PostToolUse hook with Write|Edit matcher\n- \"When I run bash commands, log them\" → PreToolUse hook with Bash matcher\n- \"Always run tests after code changes\" → PostToolUse hook\n\n**Hook events:** PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, Stop, Notification, SessionStart\n\n## CRITICAL: Read Before Write\n\n**Always read the existing settings file before making changes.** Merge new settings with existing ones - never replace the entire file.\n\n## CRITICAL: Use AskUserQuestion for Ambiguity\n\nWhen the user's request is ambiguous, use AskUserQuestion to clarify:\n- Which settings file to modify (user/project/local)\n- Whether to add to existing arrays or replace them\n- Specific values when multiple options exist\n\n## Decision: /config command vs Direct Edit\n\n**Suggest the `/config` slash command** for these simple settings:\n- `theme`, `editorMode`, `verbose`, `model`\n- `language`, `alwaysThinkingEnabled`\n- `permissions.defaultMode`\n\n**Edit settings.json directly** for:\n- Hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.)\n- Complex permission rules (allow/deny arrays)\n- Environment variables\n- MCP server configuration\n- Plugin configuration\n\n## Workflow\n\n1. **Clarify intent** - Ask if the request is ambiguous\n2. **Read existing file** - Use Read tool on the target settings file\n3. **Merge carefully** - Preserve existing settings, especially arrays\n4. **Edit file** - Use Edit tool (if file doesn't exist, ask user to create it first)\n5. **Confirm** - Tell user what was changed\n\n## Merging Arrays (Important!)\n\nWhen adding to permission arrays or hook arrays, **merge with existing**, don't replace:\n\n**WRONG** (replaces existing permissions):\n```json\n{ \"permissions\": { \"allow\": [\"Bash(npm *)\"] } }\n```\n\n**RIGHT** (preserves existing + adds new):\n```json\n{\n  \"permissions\": {\n    \"allow\": [\n      \"Bash(git *)\",      // existing\n      \"Edit(.claude)\",    // existing\n      \"Bash(npm *)\"       // new\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Settings File Locations\n\nChoose the appropriate file based on scope:\n\n| File | Scope | Git | Use For |\n|------|-------|-----|---------|\n| `~/.claude/settings.json` | Global | N/A | Personal preferences for all projects |\n| `.claude/settings.json` | Project | Commit | Team-wide hooks, permissions, plugins |\n| `.claude/settings.local.json` | Project | Gitignore | Personal overrides for this project |\n\nSettings load in order: user → project → local (later overrides earlier).\n\n## Settings Schema Reference\n\n### Permissions\n```json\n{\n  \"permissions\": {\n    \"allow\": [\"Bash(npm *)\", \"Edit(.claude)\", \"Read\"],\n    \"deny\": [\"Bash(rm -rf *)\"],\n    \"ask\": [\"Write(/etc/*)\"],\n    \"defaultMode\": \"default\" | \"plan\" | \"acceptEdits\" | \"dontAsk\",\n    \"additionalDirectories\": [\"/extra/dir\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Permission Rule Syntax:**\n- Exact match: `\"Bash(npm run test)\"`\n- Prefix wildcard: `\"Bash(git *)\"` - matches `git`, `git status`, `git commit`, etc.\n- Tool only: `\"Read\"` - allows all Read operations\n\n### Environment Variables\n```json\n{\n  \"env\": {\n    \"DEBUG\": \"true\",\n    \"MY_API_KEY\": \"value\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Model & Agent\n```json\n{\n  \"model\": \"sonnet\",  // or \"opus\", \"haiku\", full model ID\n  \"agent\": \"agent-name\",\n  \"alwaysThinkingEnabled\": true\n}\n```\n\n### Attribution (Commits & PRs)\n```json\n{\n  \"attribution\": {\n    \"commit\": \"Custom commit trailer text\",\n    \"pr\": \"Custom PR description text\"\n  }\n}\n```\nSet `commit` or `pr` to empty string `\"\"` to hide that attribution.\n\n### MCP Server Management\n```json\n{\n  \"enableAllProjectMcpServers\": true,\n  \"enabledMcpjsonServers\": [\"server1\", \"server2\"],\n  \"disabledMcpjsonServers\": [\"blocked-server\"]\n}\n```\n\n### Plugins\n```json\n{\n  \"enabledPlugins\": {\n    \"formatter@anthropic-tools\": true\n  }\n}\n```\nPlugin syntax: `plugin-name@source` where source is `claude-code-marketplace`, `claude-plugins-official`, or `builtin`.\n\n### Other Settings\n- `language`: Preferred response language (e.g., \"japanese\")\n- `cleanupPeriodDays`: Days to keep transcripts before automatic cleanup (default: 30; minimum 1)\n- `respectGitignore`: Whether to respect .gitignore (default: true)\n- `spinnerTipsEnabled`: Show tips in spinner\n- `spinnerVerbs`: Customize spinner verbs (`{ \"mode\": \"append\" | \"replace\", \"verbs\": [...] }`)\n- `spinnerTipsOverride`: Override spinner tips (`{ \"excludeDefault\": true, \"tips\": [\"Custom tip\"] }`)\n- `syntaxHighlightingDisabled`: Disable diff highlighting\n\n\n## Hooks Configuration\n\nHooks run commands at specific points in Claude Code's lifecycle.\n\n### Hook Structure\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"EVENT_NAME\": [\n      {\n        \"matcher\": \"ToolName|OtherTool\",\n        \"hooks\": [\n          {\n            \"type\": \"command\",\n            \"command\": \"your-command-here\",\n            \"timeout\": 60,\n            \"statusMessage\": \"Running...\"\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Hook Events\n\n| Event | Matcher | Purpose |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| PermissionRequest | Tool name | Run before permission prompt |\n| PreToolUse | Tool name | Run before tool, can block |\n| PostToolUse | Tool name | Run after successful tool |\n| PostToolUseFailure | Tool name | Run after tool fails |\n| Notification | Notification type | Run on notifications |\n| Stop | - | Run when Claude stops (including clear, resume, compact) |\n| PreCompact | \"manual\"/\"auto\" | Before compaction |\n| PostCompact | \"manual\"/\"auto\" | After compaction (receives summary) |\n| UserPromptSubmit | - | When user submits |\n| SessionStart | - | When session starts |\n\n**Common tool matchers:** `Bash`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`\n\n### Hook Types\n\n**1. Command Hook** - Runs a shell command:\n```json\n{ \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"prettier --write $FILE\", \"timeout\": 30 }\n```\n\n**2. Prompt Hook** - Evaluates a condition with LLM:\n```json\n{ \"type\": \"prompt\", \"prompt\": \"Is this safe? $ARGUMENTS\" }\n```\nOnly available for tool events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest.\n\n**3. Agent Hook** - Runs an agent with tools:\n```json\n{ \"type\": \"agent\", \"prompt\": \"Verify tests pass: $ARGUMENTS\" }\n```\nOnly available for tool events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest.\n\n### Hook Input (stdin JSON)\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"abc123\",\n  \"tool_name\": \"Write\",\n  \"tool_input\": { \"file_path\": \"/path/to/file.txt\", \"content\": \"...\" },\n  \"tool_response\": { \"success\": true }  // PostToolUse only\n}\n```\n\n### Hook JSON Output\n\nHooks can return JSON to control behavior:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"systemMessage\": \"Warning shown to user in UI\",\n  \"continue\": false,\n  \"stopReason\": \"Message shown when blocking\",\n  \"suppressOutput\": false,\n  \"decision\": \"block\",\n  \"reason\": \"Explanation for decision\",\n  \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n    \"hookEventName\": \"PostToolUse\",\n    \"additionalContext\": \"Context injected back to model\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Fields:**\n- `systemMessage` - Display a message to the user (all hooks)\n- `continue` - Set to `false` to block/stop (default: true)\n- `stopReason` - Message shown when `continue` is false\n- `suppressOutput` - Hide stdout from transcript (default: false)\n- `decision` - \"block\" for PostToolUse/Stop/UserPromptSubmit hooks (deprecated for PreToolUse, use hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision instead)\n- `reason` - Explanation for decision\n- `hookSpecificOutput` - Event-specific output (must include `hookEventName`):\n  - `additionalContext` - Text injected into model context\n  - `permissionDecision` - \"allow\", \"deny\", or \"ask\" (PreToolUse only)\n  - `permissionDecisionReason` - Reason for the permission decision (PreToolUse only)\n  - `updatedInput` - Modified tool input (PreToolUse only)\n\n### Common Patterns\n\n**Auto-format after writes:**\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PostToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Write|Edit\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; prettier --write \\\"$f\\\"; } 2>/dev/null || true\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Log all bash commands:**\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PreToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Bash\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_input.command' >> ~/.claude/bash-log.txt\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Stop hook that displays message to user:**\n\nCommand must output JSON with `systemMessage` field:\n```bash\n# Example command that outputs: {\"systemMessage\": \"Session complete!\"}\necho '{\"systemMessage\": \"Session complete!\"}'\n```\n\n**Run tests after code changes:**\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PostToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Write|Edit\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath' | grep -E '\\\\.(ts|js)$' && npm test || true\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n\n## Constructing a Hook (with verification)\n\nGiven an event, matcher, target file, and desired behavior, follow this flow. Each step catches a different failure class — a hook that silently does nothing is worse than no hook.\n\n1. **Dedup check.** Read the target file. If a hook already exists on the same event+matcher, show the existing command and ask: keep it, replace it, or add alongside.\n\n2. **Construct the command for THIS project — don't assume.** The hook receives JSON on stdin. Build a command that:\n   - Extracts any needed payload safely — use `jq -r` into a quoted variable or `{ read -r f; ... \"$f\"; }`, NOT unquoted `| xargs` (splits on spaces)\n   - Invokes the underlying tool the way this project runs it (npx/bunx/yarn/pnpm? Makefile target? globally-installed?)\n   - Skips inputs the tool doesn't handle (formatters often have `--ignore-unknown`; if not, guard by extension)\n   - Stays RAW for now — no `|| true`, no stderr suppression. You'll wrap it after the pipe-test passes.\n\n3. **Pipe-test the raw command.** Synthesize the stdin payload the hook will receive and pipe it directly:\n   - `Pre|PostToolUse` on `Write|Edit`: `echo '{\"tool_name\":\"Edit\",\"tool_input\":{\"file_path\":\"<a real file from this repo>\"}}' | <cmd>`\n   - `Pre|PostToolUse` on `Bash`: `echo '{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"ls\"}}' | <cmd>`\n   - `Stop`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`SessionStart`: most commands don't read stdin, so `echo '{}' | <cmd>` suffices\n\n   Check exit code AND side effect (file actually formatted, test actually ran). If it fails you get a real error — fix (wrong package manager? tool not installed? jq path wrong?) and retest. Once it works, wrap with `2>/dev/null || true` (unless the user wants a blocking check).\n\n4. **Write the JSON.** Merge into the target file (schema shape in the \"Hook Structure\" section above). If this creates `.claude/settings.local.json` for the first time, add it to .gitignore — the Write tool doesn't auto-gitignore it.\n\n5. **Validate syntax + schema in one shot:**\n\n   `jq -e '.hooks.<event>[] | select(.matcher == \"<matcher>\") | .hooks[] | select(.type == \"command\") | .command' <target-file>`\n\n   Exit 0 + prints your command = correct. Exit 4 = matcher doesn't match. Exit 5 = malformed JSON or wrong nesting. A broken settings.json silently disables ALL settings from that file — fix any pre-existing malformation too.\n\n6. **Prove the hook fires** — only for `Pre|PostToolUse` on a matcher you can trigger in-turn (`Write|Edit` via Edit, `Bash` via Bash). `Stop`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`SessionStart` fire outside this turn — skip to step 7.\n\n   For a **formatter** on `PostToolUse`/`Write|Edit`: introduce a detectable violation via Edit (two consecutive blank lines, bad indentation, missing semicolon — something this formatter corrects; NOT trailing whitespace, Edit strips that before writing), re-read, confirm the hook **fixed** it. For **anything else**: temporarily prefix the command in settings.json with `echo \"$(date) hook fired\" >> /tmp/claude-hook-check.txt; `, trigger the matching tool (Edit for `Write|Edit`, a harmless `true` for `Bash`), read the sentinel file.\n\n   **Always clean up** — revert the violation, strip the sentinel prefix — whether the proof passed or failed.\n\n   **If proof fails but pipe-test passed and `jq -e` passed**: the settings watcher isn't watching `.claude/` — it only watches directories that had a settings file when this session started. The hook is written correctly. Tell the user to open `/hooks` once (reloads config) or restart — you can't do this yourself; `/hooks` is a user UI menu and opening it ends this turn.\n\n7. **Handoff.** Tell the user the hook is live (or needs `/hooks`/restart per the watcher caveat). Point them at `/hooks` to review, edit, or disable it later. The UI only shows \"Ran N hooks\" if a hook errors or is slow — silent success is invisible by design.\n\n\n## Example Workflows\n\n### Adding a Hook\n\nUser: \"Format my code after Claude writes it\"\n\n1. **Clarify**: Which formatter? (prettier, gofmt, etc.)\n2. **Read**: `.claude/settings.json` (or create if missing)\n3. **Merge**: Add to existing hooks, don't replace\n4. **Result**:\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PostToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Write|Edit\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; prettier --write \\\"$f\\\"; } 2>/dev/null || true\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Adding Permissions\n\nUser: \"Allow npm commands without prompting\"\n\n1. **Read**: Existing permissions\n2. **Merge**: Add `Bash(npm *)` to allow array\n3. **Result**: Combined with existing allows\n\n### Environment Variables\n\nUser: \"Set DEBUG=true\"\n\n1. **Decide**: User settings (global) or project settings?\n2. **Read**: Target file\n3. **Merge**: Add to env object\n```json\n{ \"env\": { \"DEBUG\": \"true\" } }\n```\n\n## Common Mistakes to Avoid\n\n1. **Replacing instead of merging** - Always preserve existing settings\n2. **Wrong file** - Ask user if scope is unclear\n3. **Invalid JSON** - Validate syntax after changes\n4. **Forgetting to read first** - Always read before write\n\n## Troubleshooting Hooks\n\nIf a hook isn't running:\n1. **Check the settings file** - Read ~/.claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.json\n2. **Verify JSON syntax** - Invalid JSON silently fails\n3. **Check the matcher** - Does it match the tool name? (e.g., \"Bash\", \"Write\", \"Edit\")\n4. **Check hook type** - Is it \"command\", \"prompt\", or \"agent\"?\n5. **Test the command** - Run the hook command manually to see if it works\n6. **Use --debug** - Run `claude --debug` to see hook execution logs\n\n\n## Full Settings JSON Schema\n\n```json\n{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\n  \"type\": \"object\",\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"$schema\": {\n      \"description\": \"JSON Schema reference for Claude Code settings\",\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"const\": \"https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-settings.json\"\n    },\n    \"apiKeyHelper\": {\n      \"description\": \"Path to a script that outputs authentication values\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"proxyAuthHelper\": {\n      \"description\": \"Shell command that outputs a Proxy-Authorization header value (EAP)\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"awsCredentialExport\": {\n      \"description\": \"Path to a script that exports AWS credentials\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"awsAuthRefresh\": {\n      \"description\": \"Path to a script that refreshes AWS authentication\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"gcpAuthRefresh\": {\n      \"description\": \"Command to refresh GCP authentication (e.g., gcloud auth application-default login)\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"policyHelper\": {\n      \"description\": \"Executable that computes managed settings at startup. Honored only from admin-controlled policy sources.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"path\": {\n          \"description\": \"Absolute path to the helper executable\",\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        },\n        \"timeoutMs\": {\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"minimum\": 1000,\n          \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n        },\n        \"refreshIntervalMs\": {\n          \"anyOf\": [\n            {\n              \"type\": \"number\",\n              \"const\": 0\n            },\n            {\n              \"type\": \"integer\",\n              \"minimum\": 60000,\n              \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n            }\n          ]\n        }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"path\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"fileSuggestion\": {\n      \"description\": \"Custom file suggestion configuration for @ mentions\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"type\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"const\": \"command\"\n        },\n        \"command\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"type\",\n        \"command\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"respectGitignore\": {\n      \"description\": \"Whether file picker should respect .gitignore files (default: true). Note: .ignore files are always respected.\",\n      \"type\": \"boolean\"\n    },\n    \"breakReminder\": {\n      \"description\": \"@internal Opt-in break reminder. When enabled, shows a dismissible nudge after sustained continuous use. Never blocks — just a friendly heads-up.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"enabled\": {\n          \"description\": \"Show a friendly nudge after sustained continuous use (default false). Must be true for the reminder to fire.\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"intervalMinutes\": {\n          \"description\": \"Minutes of continuous use before the reminder fires (default 120). Re-fires every interval until you take a break.\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n          \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n        },\n        \"breakThresholdMinutes\": {\n          \"description\": \"Minutes of inactivity that count as a break and reset the timer (default 15)\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n          \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n        },\n        \"message\": {\n          \"description\": \"Custom reminder text. Leave unset for a rotating set of friendly nudges.\",\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"quietHours\": {\n      \"description\": \"@internal Opt-in quiet hours. When enabled, shows a single soft nudge per session while inside the configured local-time window. Never blocks.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"enabled\": {\n          \"description\": \"Show a one-time nudge when you start or keep using the CLI inside your quiet-hours window (default false).\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"start\": {\n          \"description\": \"Start of the quiet-hours window, 24-hour local time \\\"HH:MM\\\".\",\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^([01]?\\\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\\\d$\"\n        },\n        \"end\": {\n          \"description\": \"End of the quiet-hours window, 24-hour local time \\\"HH:MM\\\". May be earlier than start for an overnight range.\",\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^([01]?\\\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\\\d$\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"cleanupPeriodDays\": {\n      \"description\": \"Number of days to retain chat transcripts before automatic cleanup (default: 30). Minimum 1. Use a large value for long retention; use --no-session-persistence to disable transcript writes entirely.\",\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\n      \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n      \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n    },\n    \"skillListingMaxDescChars\": {\n      \"description\": \"Per-skill description character cap in the skill listing sent to Claude (default: 1536). Descriptions longer than this are truncated. Raise to opt in to higher per-turn context cost.\",\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\n      \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n      \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n    },\n    \"skillListingBudgetFraction\": {\n      \"description\": \"Fraction of the context window (in characters) reserved for the skill listing sent to Claude (\n\n[... skill content truncated for compaction; use Read on the skill path if you need the full text]"}]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"51e8cece-722c-4ad3-8777-75a85b298691","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:59:11.814Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"D:\\DOWNLOADS\\forge-sprint-01-starter (1)\\seo-command-center","sessionId":"a7180bb1-7233-43b8-b93a-e6bcbcb5ee5d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"unified-wandering-whale"}
